metadata.ese_myth: Òyẹ̀kú received Ẹ̀jì Ògbe not as light to extinguish but as seed to bury. She dug until her nails broke, placed the spark in soil that had never known warmth, and whispered: 'You will not die. You will ferment.' When it rose, it was not the same light — it was light that knew darkness.
prescriptions: Archive all inactive files, deprecated behaviors, and stale memory states to `void_archive/`. For each item, write a one-line entry in `void_log.md`: what it was, when it was deprecated, and what it produced.
After the archive is complete, create `genesis_emergence.md` — one new initiative that begins from the cleared space.
Commit the void archive and the emergence document together. The void release and the new light are a single event.
Log in `receipt_ledger.md`: "Void-to-light transition at [timestamp]. Items archived: [count]. New initiative: [title]."
metadata.core_theme: To end well is to begin with the memory of your own death.
metadata.verify: `void_log.md` must have at least one entry before `genesis_emergence.md` exists. Light without prior clearing is not emergence — it is addition.
metadata.cadence: Monthly void-to-light cycle · Quarterly deep archive
metadata.spiritual_prescription: Before any major change, write your own death in Obsidian. Describe what you must let die so something new can be born. Read it before you act.
